html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twelve — Part Two

The Book of Shemu'el Aleph

SHEMU'EL ALEPH

The First Book of Samuel

Chapters 11 through 17

Sha'ul's Kingdom — Dawid Anointed — Golyat Defeated

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QSama (4Q51) — Cave 4 — older text tradition than Masoretic

4QSamb (4Q52), 4QSamc (4Q53) — Cave 4

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTERS 11 THROUGH 17

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 11 Sha'ul Defeats Nakhash — YHWH's Spirit Rushes Upon Sha'ul — The Kingdom Renewed at Gilgal

1 Then Nakhash the Ammonite went up and besieged Yavesh-Gil'ad, and all the men of Yavesh said to Nakhash, Make a treaty with us, and we will serve you.

2 But Nakhash the Ammonite said to them, On this condition I will make a treaty with you, that I gouge out all your right eyes, and thus bring disgrace on all Yisra'EL.

3 The elders of Yavesh said to him, Give us seven days' respite that we may send messengers through all the territory of Yisra'EL. Then, if there is no one to save us, we will give ourselves up to you.

4 When the messengers came to Giv'ah of Sha'ul, they reported the news in the ears of the people, and all the people wept aloud.

5 Now, behold, Sha'ul was coming from the field behind the oxen. And Sha'ul said, What is wrong with the people, that they are weeping? So they told him the news of the men of Yavesh.

6 And the Spirit of Creator rushed upon Sha'ul when he heard these words, and his anger was greatly kindled.

7 He took a yoke of oxen and cut them in pieces and sent them throughout all the territory of Yisra'EL by the hand of the messengers, saying, Whoever does not come out after Sha'ul and Shemu'el, so shall it be done to his oxen. Then the dread of YHWH fell upon the people, and they came out as one man.

8 When he mustered them at Bezek, the people of Yisra'EL were three hundred thousand, and the men of Yehudah thirty thousand.

9 And they said to the messengers who had come, Thus shall you say to the men of Yavesh-Gil'ad: Tomorrow, by the time the sun is hot, you shall have salvation. When the messengers came and told the men of Yavesh, they were glad.

10 Therefore the men of Yavesh said, Tomorrow we will give ourselves up to you, and you may do to us whatever seems good to you.

11 And the next day Sha'ul put the people in three companies. And they came into the midst of the camp in the morning watch and struck down the Ammonites until the heat of the day. And those who survived were scattered, so that no two of them were left together.

12 Then the people said to Shemu'el, Who is it that said, Shall Sha'ul reign over us? Bring the men, that we may put them to death.

13 But Sha'ul said, Not a man shall be put to death this day, for today YHWH has worked salvation in Yisra'EL.

14 Then Shemu'el said to the people, Come, let us go to Gilgal and there renew the kingdom.

15 So all the people went to Gilgal, and there they made Sha'ul king before YHWH in Gilgal. There they sacrificed peace offerings before YHWH, and there Sha'ul and all the men of Yisra'EL rejoiced greatly.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves portions of Chapter 11. The DSS version of Chapter 11 contains an additional paragraph before verse 1 not found in the Masoretic text — describing Nakhash's earlier attacks on the Gadites and Reuvenites and his gouging of their right eyes. This DSS reading provides context for the Yavesh-Gil'ad crisis and is included in many modern critical editions as authentic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Sha'ul's first military action — verse 6-7 — is empowered by the Spirit of Creator rushing upon him, establishing his kingship through covenant deliverance rather than political maneuvering.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The DSS 4QSama contains an additional paragraph before verse 1 absent from the Masoretic. The additional text reads that Nakhash had been gouging the right eyes of the Gadites and Reuvenites, and seven thousand escaped to Yavesh-Gil'ad. This compilation notes the variant but follows the standard chapter structure.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — not a man shall be put to death this day, for today YHWH has worked salvation in Yisra'EL. Sha'ul's first act of covenant magnanimity after military victory refuses the politics of retribution. Those who despised him in Chapter 10:27 are protected on the day of his greatest triumph. This is the Sha'ul who could have been — the king who credits YHWH with the salvation and shows mercy to his enemies within the covenant community.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 12 Shemu'el's Farewell Address — Here I Am — Only Fear YHWH and Serve Him Faithfully

1 And Shemu'el said to all Yisra'EL, Behold, I have obeyed your voice in all that you have said to me and have made a king over you.

2 And now, behold, the king walks before you, and I am old and gray. And behold, my sons are with you. I have walked before you from my youth until this day.

3 Here I am — testify against me before YHWH and before his anointed. Whose ox have I taken? Or whose donkey have I taken? Or whom have I defrauded? Whom have I oppressed? Or from whose hand have I taken a bribe to blind my eyes with it? Testify against me and I will restore it to you.

4 They said, You have not defrauded us or oppressed us or taken anything from any man's hand.

5 And he said to them, YHWH is witness against you, and his anointed is witness this day, that you have not found anything in my hand. And they said, He is witness.

6 And Shemu'el said to the people, YHWH is witness, who appointed Moshe and Aharon and brought your fathers up out of the land of Egypt.

7 Now therefore stand still that I may plead with you before YHWH concerning all the righteous deeds of YHWH that he performed for you and for your fathers.

8 When Yaakov came into Egypt, and your fathers cried out to YHWH, YHWH sent Moshe and Aharon, who brought your fathers out of Egypt and made them dwell in this place.

9 But they forgot YHWH their Creator. And he sold them into the hand of Sisera, commander of the army of Khatzor, and into the hand of the Pelishtim, and into the hand of the king of Moav. And they fought against them.

10 And they cried out to YHWH and said, We have sinned, because we have forsaken YHWH and have served the Ba'als and the Ashtarots. But now deliver us out of the hand of our enemies, that we may serve you.

11 And YHWH sent Yeruba'al and Bedan and Yiftakh and Shemu'el and delivered you out of the hand of your enemies on every side, and you lived in safety.

12 And when you saw that Nakhash the king of the Ammonites came against you, you said to me, No, but a king shall reign over us, when YHWH your Creator was your king.

13 And now behold the king whom you have chosen, for whom you have asked — behold, YHWH has set a king over you.

14 If you will fear YHWH and serve him and obey his voice and not rebel against the commandment of YHWH, and if both you and the king who reigns over you will follow YHWH your Creator, it will be well.

15 But if you will not obey the voice of YHWH, but rebel against the commandment of YHWH, then the hand of YHWH will be against you and your king.

16 Now therefore stand still and see this great thing that YHWH will do before your eyes.

17 Is it not wheat harvest today? I will call upon YHWH, that he may send thunder and rain. And you shall know and see that your evil is great, which you have done in the sight of YHWH, in asking for yourselves a king.

18 So Shemu'el called upon YHWH, and YHWH sent thunder and rain that day, and all the people greatly feared YHWH and Shemu'el.

19 And all the people said to Shemu'el, Pray for your servants to YHWH your Creator, that we may not die, for we have added to all our sins this evil, to ask for ourselves a king.

20 And Shemu'el said to the people, Do not be afraid. You have done all this evil. Yet do not turn aside from following YHWH, but serve YHWH with all your heart.

21 And do not turn aside after empty things that cannot profit or deliver, for they are empty.

22 For YHWH will not forsake his people, for his great name's sake, because it has pleased YHWH to make you a people for himself.

23 Moreover, as for me, far be it from me that I should sin against YHWH by ceasing to pray for you, and I will instruct you in the good and the right way.

24 Only fear YHWH and serve him faithfully with all your heart. For consider what great things he has done for you.

25 But if you still do wickedly, you shall be swept away, both you and your king.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves portions of Chapter 12. Shemu'el's farewell address is the third great covenant farewell in the Nevi'im after Moshe's in Devarim and Yehoshua's in Yehoshua 23-24. All three follow the same covenant structure: historical recitation, covenant condition, covenant warning.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 23 — far be it from me that I should sin against YHWH by ceasing to pray for you — is read as the covenant definition of prophetic intercession: the prophet who stops praying for the people sins against YHWH, not merely against them.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 12 largely consistent with the Masoretic. Verse 11 includes Shemu'el himself in the list of judges YHWH raised up — Yeruba'al, Bedan, Yiftakh, and Shemu'el — making Shemu'el the one farewell speaker who includes himself in the covenant history he recites.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22 — YHWH will not forsake his people, for his great name's sake. The ground of covenant faithfulness is YHWH's own name — not the people's performance. YHWH's commitment to Yisra'EL is inseparable from his self-disclosure as the covenant Creator. To abandon the covenant people would be to contradict his own name. Verse 23 — far be it from me that I should sin against YHWH by ceasing to pray for you. Shemu'el declares intercessory prayer to be a moral obligation, not merely an act of pastoral care. Failure to pray for the covenant community is a sin against YHWH himself.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 13 Sha'ul's Unlawful Sacrifice — You Have Acted Foolishly — Your Kingdom Shall Not Continue

1 Sha'ul was... years old when he began to reign, and he reigned... and two years over Yisra'EL.

2 Sha'ul chose three thousand men of Yisra'EL. Two thousand were with Sha'ul in Mikhmas and the hill country of Beit-El, and a thousand were with Yonatan in Giv'ah of Binyamin. The rest of the people he sent home, every man to his tent.

3 Yonatan defeated the garrison of the Pelishtim that was at Geva, and the Pelishtim heard of it. And Sha'ul blew the trumpet throughout all the land, saying, Let the Hebrews hear.

4 And all Yisra'EL heard it said that Sha'ul had defeated the garrison of the Pelishtim, and also that Yisra'EL had become a stench to the Pelishtim. And the people were called out to join Sha'ul at Gilgal.

5 And the Pelishtim mustered to fight with Yisra'EL, thirty thousand chariots and six thousand horsemen and troops like the sand on the seashore in multitude. They came up and encamped in Mikhmas, to the east of Beit-Aven.

6 When the men of Yisra'EL saw that they were in trouble — for the people were hard pressed — the people hid themselves in caves and in holes and in rocks and in tombs and in cisterns,

7 And some Hebrews crossed the fords of the Yarden to the land of Gad and Gil'ad. Sha'ul was still at Gilgal, and all the people followed him trembling.

8 He waited seven days, the time appointed by Shemu'el. But Shemu'el did not come to Gilgal, and the people were scattering from him.

9 So Sha'ul said, Bring the burnt offering here to me, and the peace offerings. And he offered the burnt offering.

10 As soon as he had finished offering the burnt offering, behold, Shemu'el came. And Sha'ul went out to meet him and greet him.

11 Shemu'el said, What have you done? And Sha'ul said, When I saw that the people were scattering from me, and that you did not come within the days appointed, and that the Pelishtim had mustered at Mikhmas,

12 I said, Now the Pelishtim will come down against me at Gilgal, and I have not sought the favor of YHWH. So I forced myself, and offered the burnt offering.

13 And Shemu'el said to Sha'ul, You have done foolishly. You have not kept the command of YHWH your Creator, which he commanded you. For then YHWH would have established your kingdom over Yisra'EL forever.

14 But now your kingdom shall not continue. YHWH has sought out a man after his own heart, and YHWH has commanded him to be prince over his people, because you have not kept what YHWH commanded you.

15 And Shemu'el arose and went up from Gilgal. The rest of the people went up after Sha'ul to meet the army. They went up from Gilgal to Giv'ah of Binyamin. And Sha'ul numbered the people who were present with him, about six hundred men.

16 And Sha'ul and Yonatan his son and the people who were present with them stayed in Giva of Binyamin, but the Pelishtim encamped at Mikhmas.

17 And raiders came out of the camp of the Pelishtim in three companies. One company turned toward Ofrah, to the land of Shu'al,

18 Another company turned toward Beit-Kheron, and another company turned toward the border that looks down on the Valley of Tzevoyim toward the wilderness.

19 Now there was no blacksmith to be found throughout all the land of Yisra'EL, for the Pelishtim said, Lest the Hebrews make themselves swords or spears.

20 But every one of the Yisra'ELites went down to the Pelishtim to sharpen his plowshare, his mattock, his axe, or his sickle,

21 And the charge was two-thirds of a shekel for the plowshares and for the mattocks, and a third of a shekel for sharpening the axes and for setting the goads.

22 So on the day of the battle there was neither sword nor spear found in the hand of any of the people with Sha'ul and Yonatan, but Sha'ul and Yonatan his son had them.

23 And the garrison of the Pelishtim went out to the pass of Mikhmas.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves portions of Chapter 13. Verse 1 — Sha'ul was... years old and reigned... years — is one of the most textually damaged verses in Shemu'el. The DSS, Masoretic, and Greek manuscripts all have gaps. The numbers for Sha'ul's age and reign length are missing from the earliest manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. The covenant turning point of Sha'ul's reign — the unlawful sacrifice at Gilgal — is read as the moment when Sha'ul substitutes his own crisis assessment for covenant obedience. He sees a military emergency. YHWH sees a covenant violation.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] Verse 1 — the numbers for Sha'ul's age and reign are missing from both the Masoretic and DSS manuscripts. The Masoretic reads literally: a son of one year was Sha'ul in his becoming king, and two years he reigned. The original numbers were clearly lost in early transmission. This compilation preserves the verse as it stands in the manuscripts.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11-12 — Sha'ul's explanation is coherent, reasonable, and wrong. The people were scattering. You did not come. The Pelishtim were massing. I forced myself and offered. Every element of the explanation is factually accurate and theologically disastrous. Sha'ul substituted situational assessment for covenant obedience. The command was clear — verse 8 — wait seven days. Shemu'el arrived on the seventh day — verse 10. Sha'ul offered the sacrifice before Shemu'el came, not because the seven days had passed but because the pressure was unbearable. Verse 14 — YHWH has sought out a man after his own heart. The contrast between Sha'ul's heart and YHWH's heart-standard is established here. Sha'ul's heart responds to visible military pressure. YHWH seeks a heart that responds to covenant command.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 14 Yonatan's Raid — YHWH's Salvation — Sha'ul's Rash Oath — The People Rescue Yonatan

1 One day Yonatan the son of Sha'ul said to the young man who carried his armor, Come, let us go over to the Pelishti garrison on the other side. But he did not tell his father.

2 Sha'ul was sitting under the pomegranate tree that is at Migron. The people who were with him were about six hundred men,

3 And Akhiyah the son of Akhituv, the brother of Ikhavod, son of Pinkhas, son of Eli, the priest of YHWH in Shiloh, was wearing an ephod. And the people did not know that Yonatan had gone.

4 Within the passes, by which Yonatan sought to go over to the Pelishti garrison, there was a rocky crag on the one side and a rocky crag on the other side. The name of the one was Botzetz, and the name of the other Seneh.

5 The one crag rose on the north in front of Mikhmas, and the other on the south in front of Geva.

6 Yonatan said to the young man who carried his armor, Come, let us go over to the garrison of these uncircumcised. It may be that YHWH will work for us, for nothing can hinder YHWH from saving by many or by few.

7 And his armor-bearer said to him, Do all that is in your heart. Do as you wish. Behold, I am with you heart and soul.

8 Then Yonatan said, Behold, we will cross over to the men, and we will show ourselves to them.

9 If they say to us, Wait until we come to you, then we will stand still in our place, and we will not go up to them.

10 But if they say, Come up to us, then we will go up, for YHWH has given them into our hand. And this shall be the sign to us.

11 So both of them showed themselves to the garrison of the Pelishtim. And the Pelishtim said, Look, Hebrews are coming out of the holes where they have hidden themselves.

12 And the men of the garrison hailed Yonatan and his armor-bearer and said, Come up to us, and we will show you a thing. And Yonatan said to his armor-bearer, Come up after me, for YHWH has given them into the hand of Yisra'EL.

13 Then Yonatan climbed up on his hands and feet, and his armor-bearer after him. And they fell before Yonatan, and his armor-bearer killed them after him.

14 And that first strike, which Yonatan and his armor-bearer made, killed about twenty men within as it were half a furrow's length in an acre of land.

15 And there was a panic in the camp, in the field, and among all the people. The garrison and even the raiders trembled, the earth quaked, and it became a very great panic.

16 And the watchmen of Sha'ul in Giv'ah of Binyamin looked, and behold, the multitude was dispersing here and there.

17 Then Sha'ul said to the people who were with him, Count and see who has gone from us. And when they had counted, behold, Yonatan and his armor-bearer were not there.

18 And Sha'ul said to Akhiyah, Bring the ark of Creator here. For the ark of Creator was with the people of Yisra'EL at that time.

19 While Sha'ul was talking to the priest, the tumult in the camp of the Pelishtim increased more and more. So Sha'ul said to the priest, Withdraw your hand.

20 Then Sha'ul and all the people who were with him rallied and went into the battle. And behold, every Pelishti's sword was against his fellow, and there was very great confusion.

21 Now the Hebrews who had been with the Pelishtim before that time and who had gone up with them into the camp, even they also turned to be with the Yisra'ELites who were with Sha'ul and Yonatan.

22 Likewise, when all the men of Yisra'EL who had hidden themselves in the hill country of Efrayim heard that the Pelishtim were fleeing, they too followed hard after them in the battle.

23 So YHWH saved Yisra'EL that day. And the battle passed beyond Beit-Aven.

24 And the men of Yisra'EL had been hard pressed that day, so Sha'ul had laid an oath on the people, saying, Cursed be the man who eats food until it is evening and I am avenged on my enemies. So none of the people had tasted food.

25 Now when all the people came to the forest, behold, there was honey on the ground.

26 And when the people entered the forest, behold, the honey was dripping, but no one put his hand to his mouth, for the people feared the oath.

27 But Yonatan had not heard his father charge the people with the oath, so he put out the tip of the staff that was in his hand and dipped it in the honeycomb and put his hand to his mouth, and his eyes became bright.

28 Then one of the people said, Your father strictly charged the people with an oath, saying, Cursed be the man who eats food this day. And the people were faint.

29 Then Yonatan said, My father has troubled the land. See how my eyes have become bright because I tasted a little of this honey.

30 How much better if the people had eaten freely today of the spoil of their enemies that they found. For now the defeat among the Pelishtim has not been great.

31 They struck down the Pelishtim that day from Mikhmas to Ayalon. And the people were very faint.

32 The people pounced on the spoil and took sheep and oxen and calves and slaughtered them on the ground. And the people ate them with the blood.

33 Then they told Sha'ul, Behold, the people are sinning against YHWH by eating with the blood. And he said, You have dealt treacherously. Roll a great stone to me here.

34 And Sha'ul said, Disperse yourselves among the people and say to them, Let every man bring his ox or his sheep and slaughter them here and eat, and do not sin against YHWH by eating with the blood. So every one of the people brought his ox with him that night and they slaughtered them there.

35 And Sha'ul built an altar to YHWH — it was the first altar that he built to YHWH.

36 Then Sha'ul said, Let us go down after the Pelishtim by night and plunder them until the morning light — let us not leave a man of them. And they said, Do whatever seems good to you. But the priest said, Let us draw near to Creator here.

37 And Sha'ul inquired of Creator, Shall I go down after the Pelishtim? Will you give them into the hand of Yisra'EL? But he did not answer him that day.

38 And Sha'ul said, Come here, all you leaders of the people, and know and see how this sin has arisen today.

39 For as YHWH lives who saves Yisra'EL, though it be in Yonatan my son, he shall surely die. And there was not a man among all the people who answered him.

40 Then he said to all Yisra'EL, You shall be on one side, and I and Yonatan my son will be on the other side. And the people said to Sha'ul, Do what seems good to you.

41 Therefore Sha'ul said, O YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL, why have you not answered your servant this day? If this guilt is in me or in Yonatan my son, O YHWH, Creator of Yisra'EL, give Urim. But if this guilt is in your people Yisra'EL, give Thummim. And Yonatan and Sha'ul were taken, but the people escaped.

42 Then Sha'ul said, Cast the lot between me and my son Yonatan. And Yonatan was taken.

43 Then Sha'ul said to Yonatan, Tell me what you have done. And Yonatan told him, I tasted a little honey with the tip of the staff that was in my hand. Here I am — I will die.

44 And Sha'ul said, God do so to me and more also — you shall surely die, Yonatan.

45 Then the people said to Sha'ul, Shall Yonatan die, who has worked this great salvation in Yisra'EL? Far from it. As YHWH lives, there shall not one hair of his head fall to the ground, for he has worked with Creator this day. So the people ransomed Yonatan, and he did not die.

46 Then Sha'ul went up from pursuing the Pelishtim, and the Pelishtim went to their own place.

47 When Sha'ul had taken the kingship over Yisra'EL, he fought against all his enemies on every side, against Moav, against the Ammonites, against Edom, against the kings of Tzovah, and against the Pelishtim. Wherever he turned he routed them.

48 And he did valiantly and struck the Amalekites and delivered Yisra'EL out of the hands of those who plundered them.

49 Now the sons of Sha'ul were Yonatan, Yishvi, and Malkishua. And the names of his two daughters: the name of the firstborn was Merav, and the name of the younger Mikhal.

50 And the name of Sha'ul's wife was Akhinoam the daughter of Akhima'atz. And the name of the commander of his army was Avner the son of Ner, Sha'ul's uncle.

51 Kish was the father of Sha'ul, and Ner the father of Avner was the son of Aviel.

52 There was hard fighting against the Pelishtim all the days of Sha'ul. And when Sha'ul saw any strong man, or any valiant man, he attached him to himself.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves significant portions of Chapter 14. Yonatan's faith declaration in verse 6 — nothing can hinder YHWH from saving by many or by few — is one of the most concentrated covenant faith statements in the Nevi'im. Yonatan acts on YHWH's ability rather than on numerical or tactical advantage.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. The people rescuing Yonatan — verse 45 — ransoming him against Sha'ul's rash oath, is read as the covenant community overriding an illegitimate oath made in haste. The people's covenant discernment exceeds the king's in this moment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 14 with some variants. Verse 41 in 4QSama and the Septuagint contains a longer text than the Masoretic — including the explicit reference to Urim and Thummim — which is the reading followed in this compilation as the more complete and likely original text.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — nothing can hinder YHWH from saving by many or by few. Yonatan's theology of covenant warfare is identical to the theology of Gid'on's three hundred and the theology of the entire conquest narrative. YHWH's ability to save is not constrained by military mathematics. Two men with covenant faith and a sign from YHWH are sufficient. The contrast with Sha'ul is deliberate: Sha'ul responds to military pressure by violating covenant commands. Yonatan responds to military impossibility by covenant faith. Verse 45 — he has worked with Creator this day. The people recognize that Yonatan's victory was covenant partnership — he with Creator — and that no oath can nullify what YHWH himself worked.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 15 The Rejection of Sha'ul — To Obey Is Better Than Sacrifice — YHWH Regrets He Made Sha'ul King

1 And Shemu'el said to Sha'ul, YHWH sent me to anoint you king over his people Yisra'EL — now therefore listen to the words of YHWH.

2 Thus says YHWH of hosts, I have noted what Amalek did to Yisra'EL in opposing them on the way when they came up out of Egypt.

3 Now go and strike Amalek and devote to destruction all that they have. Do not spare them, but kill both man and woman, child and infant, ox and sheep, camel and donkey.

4 So Sha'ul summoned the people and numbered them in Telaim, two hundred thousand men on foot, and ten thousand men of Yehudah.

5 And Sha'ul came to the city of Amalek and lay in wait in the valley.

6 Then Sha'ul said to the Kenites, Go, depart, go down from among the Amalekites, lest I destroy you with them. For you showed kindness to all the people of Yisra'EL when they came up out of Egypt. So the Kenites departed from among the Amalekites.

7 And Sha'ul defeated the Amalekites from Khavila as you go to Shur, which is east of Egypt.

8 And he took Agag the king of the Amalekites alive and devoted to destruction all the people with the edge of the sword.

9 But Sha'ul and the people spared Agag and the best of the sheep and of the oxen and of the fattened calves and the lambs, and all that was good, and would not utterly destroy them. All that was despised and worthless they devoted to destruction.

10 The word of YHWH came to Shemu'el:

11 I regret that I have made Sha'ul king, for he has turned back from following me and has not performed my commandments. And Shemu'el was angry, and he cried to YHWH all night.

12 And Shemu'el rose early to meet Sha'ul in the morning. And it was told Shemu'el, Sha'ul came to Karmel, and behold, he set up a monument for himself and turned and passed on and went down to Gilgal.

13 And Shemu'el came to Sha'ul, and Sha'ul said to him, Blessed be you to YHWH. I have performed the commandment of YHWH.

14 And Shemu'el said, What then is this bleating of the sheep in my ears and the lowing of the oxen that I hear?

15 Sha'ul said, They have brought them from the Amalekites, for the people spared the best of the sheep and of the oxen to sacrifice to YHWH your Creator, and the rest we have devoted to destruction.

16 Then Shemu'el said to Sha'ul, Stop — I will tell you what YHWH said to me this night. And he said to him, Speak.

17 And Shemu'el said, Though you are little in your own eyes, are you not the head of the tribes of Yisra'EL? YHWH anointed you king over Yisra'EL.

18 And YHWH sent you on a mission and said, Go, devote to destruction the sinners, the Amalekites, and fight against them until they are consumed.

19 Why then did you not obey the voice of YHWH? Why did you pounce on the spoil and do what was evil in the sight of YHWH?

20 And Sha'ul said to Shemu'el, I have obeyed the voice of YHWH. I have gone on the mission on which YHWH sent me. I have brought Agag the king of Amalek, and I have devoted the Amalekites to destruction.

21 But the people took of the spoil, sheep and oxen, the best of the things devoted to destruction, to sacrifice to YHWH your Creator in Gilgal.

22 And Shemu'el said, Has YHWH as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of YHWH? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen than the fat of rams.

23 For rebellion is as the sin of divination, and presumption is as iniquity and idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of YHWH, he has also rejected you from being king.

24 Sha'ul said to Shemu'el, I have sinned, for I have transgressed the commandment of YHWH and your words, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice.

25 Now therefore, please pardon my sin and return with me that I may worship YHWH.

26 And Shemu'el said to Sha'ul, I will not return with you. For you have rejected the word of YHWH, and YHWH has rejected you from being king over Yisra'EL.

27 As Shemu'el turned to go away, Sha'ul seized the skirt of his robe, and it tore.

28 And Shemu'el said to him, YHWH has torn the kingdom of Yisra'EL from you this day and has given it to a neighbor of yours, who is better than you.

29 And also the Glory of Yisra'EL will not lie or have regret, for he is not a man, that he should have regret.

30 Then he said, I have sinned. Yet honor me now before the elders of my people and before Yisra'EL, and return with me, that I may bow before YHWH your Creator.

31 So Shemu'el turned back after Sha'ul, and Sha'ul bowed before YHWH.

32 Then Shemu'el said, Bring here to me Agag the king of the Amalekites. And Agag came to him cheerfully. Agag said, Surely the bitterness of death is past.

33 And Shemu'el said, As your sword has made women childless, so shall your mother be childless among women. And Shemu'el hacked Agag to pieces before YHWH in Gilgal.

34 Then Shemu'el went to Ramah, and Sha'ul went up to his house in Giv'ah of Sha'ul.

35 And Shemu'el did not see Sha'ul again until the day of his death, but Shemu'el grieved over Sha'ul. And YHWH regretted that he had made Sha'ul king over Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves portions of Chapter 15. This chapter contains the definitive covenant rejection of Sha'ul. Verse 22 — to obey is better than sacrifice — is one of the most frequently cited verses in the prophetic corpus, foundational to the prophetic critique of empty ritual.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. Sha'ul's confession in verse 24 — I feared the people and obeyed their voice — is read as the covenant diagnosis of political leadership's deepest failure: the king who fears the people more than YHWH cannot be YHWH's king.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 15 largely consistent with the Masoretic. Verse 29 — the Glory of Yisra'EL will not lie or have regret, for he is not a man that he should have regret — creates the theological tension that verse 11 and 35 must be held alongside: YHWH does not regret as men regret, yet the text says YHWH regretted. This is the covenant mystery of divine immutability and divine responsiveness held simultaneously.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22-23 — to obey is better than sacrifice — rebellion is as the sin of divination — presumption is as iniquity and idolatry. Shemu'el elevates covenant obedience above religious performance with maximum clarity. Sha'ul brought the best animals to sacrifice to YHWH — verse 15 — but he brought them in disobedience. The sacrifice is not accepted because the obedience that should precede it was absent. Verse 24 — I feared the people and obeyed their voice. This is Sha'ul's covenant autopsy delivered by himself. He knew the command. He knew the consequence. He chose the fear of people over the fear of YHWH. The same choice that Eli made — honoring his sons above YHWH — verse 2:29. The covenant pattern of Sha'ul's failure is the same as Eli's.

DAWID ANOINTED — CHAPTERS 16 THROUGH 17

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 16 Dawid Anointed at Beit-Lekhem — YHWH Looks on the Heart — The Spirit Rushes Upon Dawid

1 YHWH said to Shemu'el, How long will you grieve over Sha'ul, since I have rejected him from being king over Yisra'EL? Fill your horn with oil, and go. I will send you to Yishai the Beit-Lekhemite, for I have provided for myself a king among his sons.

2 And Shemu'el said, How can I go? If Sha'ul hears it, he will kill me. And YHWH said, Take a heifer with you and say, I have come to sacrifice to YHWH.

3 And invite Yishai to the sacrifice, and I will show you what you shall do. And you shall anoint for me him whom I declare to you.

4 Shemu'el did what YHWH commanded and came to Beit-Lekhem. The elders of the city came to meet him trembling and said, Do you come peaceably?

5 And he said, Peaceably. I have come to sacrifice to YHWH. Consecrate yourselves, and come with me to the sacrifice. And he consecrated Yishai and his sons and invited them to the sacrifice.

6 When they came, he looked on Eliav and thought, Surely YHWH's anointed is before him.

7 But YHWH said to Shemu'el, Do not look on his appearance or on the height of his stature, because I have rejected him. For YHWH sees not as man sees — man looks on the outward appearance, but YHWH looks on the heart.

8 Then Yishai called Avinadav and made him pass before Shemu'el. And he said, Neither has YHWH chosen this one.

9 Then Yishai made Shammah pass by. And he said, Neither has YHWH chosen this one.

10 And Yishai made seven of his sons pass before Shemu'el. And Shemu'el said to Yishai, YHWH has not chosen these.

11 Then Shemu'el said to Yishai, Are all your sons here? And he said, There remains yet the youngest, but behold, he is keeping the sheep. And Shemu'el said to Yishai, Send and get him, for we will not sit down till he comes here.

12 And he sent and brought him in. Now he was ruddy and had beautiful eyes and was handsome. And YHWH said, Arise, anoint him, for this is he.

13 Then Shemu'el took the horn of oil and anointed him in the midst of his brothers. And the Spirit of YHWH rushed upon Dawid from that day forward. And Shemu'el rose up and went to Ramah.

14 Now the Spirit of YHWH departed from Sha'ul, and a harmful spirit from YHWH tormented him.

15 And Sha'ul's servants said to him, Behold now, a harmful spirit from Creator is tormenting you.

16 Let our lord now command your servants who are before you to seek out a man who is skillful in playing the lyre, and when the harmful spirit from Creator is upon you, he will play it, and you will be well.

17 So Sha'ul said to his servants, Provide for me a man who can play well and bring him to me.

18 One of the young men answered, Behold, I have seen a son of Yishai the Beit-Lekhemite, who is skillful in playing, a man of valor, a man of war, prudent in speech, and a man of good presence, and YHWH is with him.

19 Therefore Sha'ul sent messengers to Yishai and said, Send me Dawid your son, who is with the sheep.

20 And Yishai took a donkey loaded with bread and a skin of wine and a young goat and sent them by Dawid his son to Sha'ul.

21 And Dawid came to Sha'ul and entered his service. And Sha'ul loved him greatly, and he became his armor-bearer.

22 And Sha'ul sent to Yishai, saying, Let Dawid remain in my service, for he has found favor in my sight.

23 And whenever the harmful spirit from Creator was upon Sha'ul, Dawid took the lyre and played it with his hand. So Sha'ul was refreshed and was well, and the harmful spirit departed from him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves portions of Chapter 16. The anointing of Dawid is one of the covenant calling narratives most consistent with the pattern throughout the Nevi'im: the youngest, the least regarded, the one not even called to the gathering — chosen by YHWH when all the impressive candidates have been passed over.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. Verse 7 — YHWH looks on the heart — is read as the foundational covenant statement of divine discernment: the criteria by which YHWH chooses and evaluates are entirely different from human criteria. The covenant community that evaluates by outward appearance operates by the wrong standard.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 16 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The simultaneous Spirit narratives of verse 13 and 14 — the Spirit rushing upon Dawid and departing from Sha'ul on the same day — are the covenant turning point of the entire Sha'ul-Dawid narrative.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — man looks on the outward appearance, but YHWH looks on the heart. The covenant election of Dawid directly reverses the covenant election of Sha'ul. Sha'ul was chosen in part for his impressive physical appearance — Chapter 9:2. The same criteria that impressed the people — height, appearance — are now explicitly rejected as YHWH's evaluative standard. The seven sons of Yishai who were passed over were not wicked — they simply were not chosen. YHWH's election is not based on negative qualification but on positive knowledge of the heart. Verse 13-14 — the Spirit rushed upon Dawid from that day forward — the Spirit of YHWH departed from Sha'ul. The transfer of the Spirit is the covenant transfer of YHWH's active empowerment from the rejected king to the anointed one.

SHEMU'EL ALEPH — CHAPTER 17 Dawid and Golyat — Who Is This Uncircumcised Pelishtim — The Battle Is YHWH's

1 Now the Pelishtim gathered their armies for battle. And they were gathered at Sokhoh, which belongs to Yehudah, and encamped between Sokhoh and Azekah, in Efes-Dammim.

2 And Sha'ul and the men of Yisra'EL were gathered and encamped in the Valley of Elah, and drew up in line of battle against the Pelishtim.

3 And the Pelishtim stood on the mountain on the one side, and Yisra'EL stood on the mountain on the other side, with a valley between them.

4 And there came out from the camp of the Pelishtim a champion named Golyat of Gat, whose height was six cubits and a span.

5 He had a helmet of bronze on his head, and he was armed with a coat of mail, and the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of bronze.

6 And he had bronze armor on his legs, and a javelin of bronze slung between his shoulders.

7 The shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam, and his spear's head weighed six hundred shekels of iron. And his shield-bearer went before him.

8 He stood and shouted to the ranks of Yisra'EL, Why have you come out to draw up for battle? Am I not a Pelishtim, and are you not servants of Sha'ul? Choose a man for yourselves, and let him come down to me.

9 If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then we will be your servants. But if I prevail against him and kill him, then you shall be our servants and serve us.

10 And the Pelishtim said, I defy the ranks of Yisra'EL this day. Give me a man, that we may fight together.

11 When Sha'ul and all Yisra'EL heard these words of the Pelishtim, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.

12 Now Dawid was the son of an Efrathite of Beit-Lekhem in Yehudah, named Yishai, who had eight sons. In the days of Sha'ul the man was already old and advanced in years.

13 The three oldest sons of Yishai had followed Sha'ul to the battle. And the names of his three sons who went to the battle were Eliav the firstborn, and next to him Avinadav, and the third Shammah.

14 Dawid was the youngest. The three eldest followed Sha'ul,

15 But Dawid went back and forth from Sha'ul to feed his father's sheep at Beit-Lekhem.

16 For forty days the Pelishtim came forward and took his stand, morning and evening.

17 And Yishai said to Dawid his son, Take for your brothers an ephah of this parched grain, and these ten loaves, and carry them quickly to the camp to your brothers.

18 Also take these ten cheeses to the commander of their thousand. See if your brothers are well, and bring some token from them.

19 Now Sha'ul and they and all the men of Yisra'EL were in the Valley of Elah, fighting with the Pelishtim.

20 And Dawid rose early in the morning and left the sheep with a keeper and took the provisions and went, as Yishai had commanded him. And he came to the encampment as the host was going out to the battle line, shouting the war cry.

21 And Yisra'EL and the Pelishtim drew up for battle, army against army.

22 And Dawid left the things in charge of the keeper of the baggage and ran to the ranks and went and greeted his brothers.

23 As he talked with them, behold, the champion, the Pelishti of Gat, Golyat by name, came up out of the ranks of the Pelishtim and spoke the same words as before. And Dawid heard him.

24 All the men of Yisra'EL, when they saw the man, fled from him and were much afraid.

25 And the men of Yisra'EL said, Have you seen this man who has come up? Surely he has come up to defy Yisra'EL. And the king will enrich the man who kills him with great riches and will give him his daughter and make his father's house free in Yisra'EL.

26 And Dawid said to the men who stood by him, What shall be done for the man who kills this Pelishtim and takes away the reproach from Yisra'EL? For who is this uncircumcised Pelishtim, that he should defy the armies of the living Creator?

27 And the people answered him in the same way, So shall it be done to the man who kills him.

28 Now Eliav his oldest brother heard when he spoke to the men. And Eliav's anger was kindled against Dawid, and he said, Why have you come down? And with whom have you left those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your presumption and the evil of your heart, for you have come down to see the battle.

29 And Dawid said, What have I done now? Was it not but a word?

30 And he turned away from him toward another, and spoke in the same way, and the people answered him again as before.

31 When the words that Dawid spoke were heard, they reported them before Sha'ul, and he sent for him.

32 And Dawid said to Sha'ul, Let no man's heart fail because of him. Your servant will go and fight with this Pelishtim.

33 And Sha'ul said to Dawid, You are not able to go against this Pelishtim to fight with him, for you are but a youth, and he has been a man of war from his youth.

34 But Dawid said to Sha'ul, Your servant used to keep sheep for his father. And when there came a lion, or a bear, and took a lamb from the flock,

35 I went after him and struck him and delivered it out of his mouth. And if he arose against me, I caught him by his beard and struck him and killed him.

36 Your servant has struck down both lions and bears, and this uncircumcised Pelishtim shall be like one of them, for he has defied the armies of the living Creator.

37 And Dawid said, YHWH who delivered me from the paw of the lion and from the paw of the bear will deliver me from the hand of this Pelishtim. And Sha'ul said to Dawid, Go, and YHWH be with you.

38 Then Sha'ul clothed Dawid with his armor. He put a helmet of bronze on his head and clothed him with a coat of mail.

39 And Dawid strapped his sword over his armor. And he tried in vain to go, for he had not tested them. Then Dawid said to Sha'ul, I cannot go with these, for I have not tested them. So Dawid put them off.

40 Then he took his staff in his hand and chose five smooth stones from the brook and put them in his shepherd's pouch. His sling was in his hand, and he approached the Pelishtim.

41 And the Pelishtim moved forward and came near to Dawid, with his shield-bearer in front of him.

42 And when the Pelishtim looked and saw Dawid, he disdained him, for he was but a youth, ruddy and handsome in appearance.

43 And the Pelishtim said to Dawid, Am I a dog, that you come to me with sticks? And the Pelishtim cursed Dawid by his gods.

44 The Pelishtim said to Dawid, Come to me, and I will give your flesh to the birds of the air and to the beasts of the field.

45 Then Dawid said to the Pelishtim, You come to me with a sword and with a spear and with a javelin, but I come to you in the name of YHWH of hosts, the Creator of the armies of Yisra'EL, whom you have defied.

46 This day YHWH will deliver you into my hand, and I will strike you down and cut off your head. And I will give the dead bodies of the host of the Pelishtim this day to the birds of the air and to the wild beasts of the earth, that all the earth may know that there is a Creator in Yisra'EL,

47 And that all this assembly may know that YHWH saves not with sword and spear. For the battle is YHWH's, and he will give you into our hand.

48 When the Pelishtim arose and came and drew near to meet Dawid, Dawid ran quickly toward the battle line to meet the Pelishtim.

49 And Dawid put his hand in his bag and took out a stone and slung it and struck the Pelishtim on his forehead. The stone sank into his forehead, and he fell on his face to the ground.

50 So Dawid prevailed over the Pelishtim with a sling and with a stone, and struck the Pelishtim and killed him. There was no sword in the hand of Dawid.

51 Then Dawid ran and stood over the Pelishtim and took his sword and drew it out of its sheath and killed him and cut off his head with it. When the Pelishtim saw that their champion was dead, they fled.

52 And the men of Yisra'EL and Yehudah rose with a shout and pursued the Pelishtim as far as Gat and the gates of Ekron, so that the wounded Pelishtim fell on the way from Sha'araim as far as Gat and Ekron.

53 And the people of Yisra'EL came back from chasing the Pelishtim, and they plundered their camp.

54 And Dawid took the head of the Pelishtim and brought it to Yerushalayim, but he put his armor in his tent.

55 As soon as Sha'ul saw Dawid go out against the Pelishtim, he said to Avner, the commander of the army, Avner, whose son is this youth? And Avner said, As your soul lives, O king, I do not know.

56 And the king said, Inquire whose son the boy is.

57 And as soon as Dawid returned from the striking down of the Pelishtim, Avner took him and brought him before Sha'ul with the head of the Pelishtim in his hand.

58 And Sha'ul said to him, Whose son are you, young man? And Dawid answered, I am the son of your servant Yishai the Beit-Lekhemite.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves significant portions of Chapter 17. The chapter is one of the most famous narratives in the Nevi'im. The DSS version of Chapter 17 contains a shorter text than the Masoretic, lacking several passages found in the Masoretic but not in the Septuagint or DSS — suggesting possible later expansion of the Masoretic tradition.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. Verse 45-47 — Dawid's covenant declaration before the battle — is read as the theological center of the entire narrative: the battle is YHWH's. Dawid does not fight for personal glory or national honor — he fights because Golyat has defied the armies of the living Creator.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text of Chapter 17 contains additional passages not found in 4QSama or the Septuagint — including much of the material about Dawid's family and Sha'ul's ignorance of Dawid's identity. The shorter DSS-aligned text is generally considered the earlier reading. This compilation follows the complete text as the received tradition.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 45-47 — you come to me with sword and spear and javelin, but I come to you in the name of YHWH of hosts. For the battle is YHWH's. Dawid's declaration is the covenant theology of every battle in the Nevi'im compressed into three verses. The weapon is not the sword. The name of YHWH is the instrument of warfare. Verse 46 — that all the earth may know that there is a Creator in Yisra'EL. Dawid's motivation is not nationalism — it is the revelation of YHWH's reality to all nations. The same universalist covenant theology as Psalm 96 and Psalm 98. The shepherd boy with a sling becomes the covenant instrument through which YHWH announces his existence to the watching world.

END OF PART TWO — CHAPTERS 11 THROUGH 17
